scoring_notes: Recurring series, which hurts the uniqueness score ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” but it's the next installment in a series that just started this spring, and each individual concert is a one-time performance. Works well for empty nesters and established families with older kids alike. The main trade-off: it's not a can't-miss event on its own, which is why it ranks second.

event_name: Brumby Hall & Garden Historic Tour and Luncheon
venue: Brumby Hall, 472 Powder Springs Street SW, Marietta
blurb:
ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚В­ГѓВ‚Г‚Вђ **Featured Event: Brumby Hall & Garden Historic Tour and Luncheon**
ГѓВѓГ‚В°ГѓВ‚Г‚ВџГѓВ‚Г‚В“ГѓВ‚Г‚В… Friday, May 1 | 11:00 AM ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В“ 1:30 PM | Brumby Hall, Marietta
ГѓВѓГ‚В°ГѓВ‚Г‚ВџГѓВ‚Г‚ВЋГѓВ‚Г‚ВџГѓВѓГ‚ВЇГѓВ‚Г‚ВёГѓВ‚Г‚ВЏ Details & tickets at [mdjonline.com](https://www.mdjonline.com/news/events-and-happenings-the-week-of-april-20/article_6cb2f1c2-b457-4945-bddf-e782d3a391f2.html)

Built in 1851 and somehow still standing ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” Brumby Hall is one of those places most East Cobb residents have driven past without ever stepping inside. That changes May 1.

This Greek Revival landmark has a story worth knowing. It was reportedly spared from Sherman's March to the Sea because of a West Point friendship between the owner and a Union officer. Whether the legend is fully true or partly polished over 175 years, it makes for a great two-hour Friday afternoon. The event includes a guided tour of the house and grounds, followed by a luncheon ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” so this is a proper outing, not just a quick walk-through.

If your idea of a good Friday involves a beautiful old house, a real story behind it, and a meal with people who appreciate that kind of thing, this one's for you. It books up ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” don't wait on it.
scoring_notes: Strong demographic fit for the older, educated, homeowner-heavy segment of the East Cobb audience ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” particularly the empty nesters and established adults who enjoy history, culture, and a proper daytime event. The uniqueness score is high because this is a once-a-year tour of a genuinely significant historic property with a compelling backstory. Audience match scores slightly lower than Taste of East Cobb because it's more niche ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” not every reader will find a historic house tour compelling ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” but for the right reader, it's the best event on the list. The Friday timing and luncheon format skews toward retired or flexible-schedule adults.

date: Saturday, April 25 or week of April 25
price: Advance food tickets available; $30 discount card usable at 20+ local restaurants
blurb:
ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚В­ГѓВ‚Г‚Вђ **Featured Event: Taste of East Cobb 2026**
ГѓВѓГ‚В°ГѓВ‚Г‚ВџГѓВ‚Г‚В“ГѓВ‚Г‚В… Coming up this week | East Cobb area restaurants & venues
ГѓВѓГ‚В°ГѓВ‚Г‚ВџГѓВ‚Г‚ВЋГѓВ‚Г‚ВџГѓВѓГ‚ВЇГѓВ‚Г‚ВёГѓВ‚Г‚ВЏ Advance food tickets + $30 discount card available | [Get Tickets & Details](https://eastcobbnews.com/taste-of-east-cobb-announces-2026-restaurants-and-exhibitors/)

This is the one East Cobb food event worth planning your week around. Taste of East Cobb brings together the best local restaurants and eateries in one stretch, letting you eat your way through the neighborhood without committing to just one place for the night. It's a great excuse to try that spot you've been meaning to get to ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” or go back to a favorite.

The $30 discount card is the move here. You get access to deals at more than 20 local restaurants and eateries, which means it pays for itself fast. Stock up and use it all month. At that price, it's basically free food.

Grab your tickets in advance and bring whoever you've been meaning to make dinner plans with. This one's right in your backyard ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” no reason to miss it.
scoring_notes: Hyper-local event happening in the newsletter's exact coverage area. East Cobb skews high-income and homeowner-heavy ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” this audience eats out, spends on food, and cares about supporting local businesses. The $30 discount card is a genuine value play even for a $118K household. Annual event but only happens once a year, and the 2026 restaurant/exhibitor announcement is fresh news this week. Strong fit across all age groups in a mixed established-families-and-empty-nesters audience.
